article_title: Tata Electronics Signs ASML as Lithography Partner for India's $11 Billion Dholera 300mm Fab
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.details:
tataelectronics.com: https://www.tataelectronics.com/w/tata-electronics-and-asml-announce-strategic-partnership-to-advance-the-semiconductor-manufacturing-ecosystem-in-india

dailysabah.com: https://www.dailysabah.com/business/tech/indias-modi-oversees-asml-tata-semiconductor-deal-on-dutch-tour/amp
thetechportal.com: https://thetechportal.com/2026/05/17/tata-electronics-partners-with-asml-for-its-11bn-chip-manufacturing-project/
tata.com: https://www.tata.com/newsroom/business/first-indian-fab-semiconductor-dholera
tataelectronics.com: https://www.tataelectronics.com/w/manufacturing-corporation-psmc-complete-landmark-agreement-for-technology-transfer-to-build-india-s-first-semiconductor-fab

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively sound semiconductor-deal coverage. All major specifics verified verbatim across snapshots: US$11 Bn investment, 'holistic suite of advanced lithography tools and solutions' phrasing, 'domestic talent, supply chain, and research initiatives' co-development scope, Thakur 'timely ramp' quote, Fouquet 'compelling opportunities' quote, Modi 'India's strides' quote, Jetten 'can help India enormously' quote, Gujarat / Modi home state framing, 50,000 WSPM, 28/40/55/90/110 nm node coverage, INR 91,000 crores (~US$11bn) original Tata-PSMC cost, PSMC 'design and construction support' / 'broad portfolio' licensing language, ASML market cap $350B and €27B revenue figures from The Tech Portal. One subordinate specific bracketed in quote marks ("$180-350 million per unit") is a compressed paraphrase — The Tech Portal actually says 'between $180 million and $350 million, depending on configuration.' Range is correct; the quote marks should not have been used. Logged in corrections.

source-0.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): ASML press release. Verifies strategic partnership framing, US$11 Bn investment, 'holistic suite of advanced lithography tools and solutions' phrasing, 'domestic talent, supply chain, and research initiatives' co-development.

source-1.status: Tata Electronics mirror of joint release. Verifies 'mobile devices' applications listing.

source-2.status: Tata.com release. Verifies Thakur 'timely ramp' quote and Fouquet 'compelling opportunities' quote verbatim.

source-3.status: Al Jazeera. Verifies Gujarat as Modi's home state, ASML as 'Europe's largest technology company by market value', Jetten naming, Hague signing context.

source-4.status: Daily Sabah. Verifies Modi 'strides in the world of semiconductors' quote and Jetten 'can help India enormously' quote verbatim, plus five-nation European tour framing.

source-5.status: The Tech Portal. Verifies 50,000 WSPM target, 28/40/55/90/110 nm node list, $350B market cap, €27B revenues. NOTE: '$180-350 million per unit' is bracketed in quote marks in the article but the source phrasing is 'between $180 million and $350 million, depending on configuration' — compressed paraphrase in quote marks. Range correct; filed correction.

source-6.status: Original 2024 Tata-PSMC fab announcement. Verifies INR 91,000 crores (~US$11bn), 'up to 50,000 wafers per month', 28/40/55/90/110 nm node coverage, 'power management IC, display drivers, microcontrollers' application list.

source-7.status: Tata-PSMC technology transfer agreement. Verifies 'design and construction support' and 'broad portfolio of technologies' licensing language.

editor_notes.content_quality: News-category (~1,047 words), Overview / What We Know / Industry Context / What We Don't Know structure. Technical and neutral tone. Clear separation between MoU specifics and broader project envelope inherited from the 2024 Tata-PSMC announcement.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the Tata-ASML Dholera MoU. The April 2026 ASML guidance article is correctly referenced as prior context (internal link), not duplicated.
concerns: Recurring failure mode (paraphrase in quote marks): '$180-350 million per unit' is in quote marks but the underlying Tech Portal text is 'between $180 million and $350 million, depending on configuration'. Filed correction.
recommendations: Add tataelectronics.com, tata.com, dailysabah.com, thetechportal.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all recurring outlets for India semiconductor coverage and primary publisher domains.

content_preview.title: Tata Electronics Signs ASML as Lithography Partner for India's $11 Billion Dholera 300mm Fab
content_preview.summary: ASML will supply the lithography toolset for Tata's Dholera 300mm fab, which Tata Electronics has costed at US$11 billion and aimed at 28nm-110nm chips for AI, automotive and mobile applications.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Tata Electronics and ASML signed a memorandum of understanding on May 16 in The Hague to make the Dutch lithography vendor the equipment partner for Tata's Dholera semiconductor fab in Gujarat, [according to ASML's joint press release](https://www.asml.com/en/news/press-releases/2026/tata-electronics-and-asml-announce-strategic-partnership). The same release puts the planned total investment at "US$11 Bn" for what will be a 300 mm (12 inch) wafer fab, and says ASML will deploy its "...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
